mnemonics: STALL: Structural hazard, True dependency, ALU dependency, Load-use dependency, Late result (control hazard)
keyPoints: Breaks instruction execution into discrete stages (IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB) to improve throughput
Hazards (structural, data, control) can cause pipeline stalls, reducing performance
The ideal speedup is equal to the number of pipeline stages, but this is rarely achieved due to hazards and overhead
commonMistakes: Assuming a 5-stage pipeline always completes 5x faster than single-cycle (ignoring hazards and overhead)
Confusing throughput (instructions completed per unit time) with latency (time to complete a single instruction)
Forgetting to account for pipeline register overhead and imbalanced stage delays in performance calculations
